seo_description: Current local time in Mumbai, India. IST (UTC+5:30) year-round, no daylight saving time. India's financial capital. Live clock, Mumbai vs New York & London.

faq.question: What time is it in Mumbai right now?
faq.answer: Mumbai, India uses India Standard Time (IST, UTC+5:30) year-round. India does not observe Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Asia/Kolkata. Mumbai operates on India Standard Time (IST/UTC+5:30) year-round. India does not observe daylight saving time, so Mumbai's offset is a constant 5 hours and 30 minutes ahead of UTC. This makes Mumbai 5.5 hours ahead of London (GMT), 10.5 hours ahead of New York (EST), and 1.5 hours behind Singapore and Hong Kong.

faq.question: What time zone is Mumbai in?
faq.answer: Mumbai is in the India Standard Time (IST) zone, which is UTC+5:30 year-round. IST is unique in using a half-hour offset — one of only a handful of time zones worldwide that is not on a full-hour boundary. The entire country of India uses a single time zone despite spanning roughly 30 degrees of longitude, from Gujarat in the west to Arunachal Pradesh in the east.

faq.question: Does Mumbai observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. India has not observed daylight saving time since 1945. Mumbai and all of India maintain IST (UTC+5:30) year-round without seasonal changes. This consistent offset makes scheduling with Indian partners straightforward — the time difference from Mumbai to any other city is always the same, regardless of time of year.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Mumbai and London?
faq.answer: Mumbai is 5 hours 30 minutes ahead of London (GMT) in winter and 4 hours 30 minutes ahead during British Summer Time (BST). When it is 9:00 AM in London (GMT), it is 2:30 PM in Mumbai. The best window for London–Mumbai calls is 9:00 AM–11:30 AM London time (2:30–5:00 PM Mumbai), before Mumbai's typical end of business.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Mumbai and Dubai?
faq.answer: Mumbai is 1 hour 30 minutes ahead of Dubai year-round. Dubai uses GST (UTC+4) while Mumbai is on IST (UTC+5:30). Neither city observes daylight saving time, so this 1.5-hour gap is constant throughout the year. When it is 9:00 AM in Dubai, it is 10:30 AM in Mumbai — making morning Dubai calls an ideal overlap window.

faq.question: What time does the Mumbai stock exchange open?
faq.answer: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) and Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) in Mumbai open at 9:15 AM IST and close at 3:30 PM IST, Monday through Friday. Pre-market trading (9:00–9:15 AM IST) precedes the main session. This translates to 3:45 AM–10:00 AM GMT for London traders and 10:45 PM–5:00 AM EST for New York traders, making Mumbai one of the first major markets to open each trading day.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Mumbai from the US?
faq.answer: From New York, Mumbai is 10.5 hours ahead during EST. The closest business-hour overlap is early morning New York: 7:00–9:00 AM EST corresponds to 5:30–7:30 PM IST in Mumbai — Mumbai's late afternoon. From Los Angeles (PST, UTC-8), Mumbai is 13.5 hours ahead — making evening PST calls (5:00–7:00 PM PST = 6:30–8:30 AM next day Mumbai) the most workable option.

faq.question: Why does India have a 30-minute time zone offset?
faq.answer: India Standard Time (UTC+5:30) uses a half-hour offset as a geographic compromise — India's geographic midpoint sits between UTC+5 and UTC+6. When the unified IST was established in 1906, the half-hour offset was chosen to keep sunrise and sunset times reasonable across the entire subcontinent. This makes IST one of the few half-hour time zones globally, alongside Iran (UTC+3:30), Afghanistan (UTC+4:30), and Nepal (UTC+5:45).
